rulings.Case Title: TYRONE ADAMS VS. GENUINE PARTS COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Re Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 24, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant BorgWarner Morse Tec Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication re: punitive damages is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. sections 437c(a), 1010.6, 12a and 12c. Plaintiff's objection based on timeliness is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ANGUS et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that Mr. Angus was not exposed to John Crane packing in or after 1970, the date by which defendant concedes it learned of the hazards of asbestos. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK BURKE VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2016 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant Campbell Soup Company's motion for summary judgment is off calendar per moving party. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: PEDRO RODRIGUEZ ET AL VS. FLOWSERVE US INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of The Goodyear Tire Rubber Companys Motion To Stay For Forum Non-Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2016 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Texas is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) If that failure is not determinate by itself, the balance of private and public interest factors likely weighs in favor of a stay as plaintiffs made no argument that such balance weighs in their favor. However, forum non conveniens is an equitable doctrine and given the evidence presented regarding Mr. Rodriguez's health, it would be inequitable to stay the action due to the likelihood that Mr. Rodriguez would die before resolving an action in either state. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
